name: Dogwood
description: The 'petals' are actually leaf-like bracts; the real flowers are the tiny cluster in the center.

name: Hydrangea
description: Bloom color shifts from blue to pink based on soil pH — uniquely so among common flowers.

name: Gardenia
description: Named for Scottish naturalist Alexander Garden; its intense scent peaks at dusk to attract moths.

name: Magnolia
description: Older than bees — magnolias evolved earlier and are pollinated by beetles.

name: St. John's Wort
description: A traditional folk remedy for melancholy — now studied as a mild antidepressant.

name: Forsythia
description: One of the first shrubs to bloom in spring — often before its leaves emerge.

name: Witch Hazel
description: Blooms in late autumn or winter; its bark is the source of the famous astringent.

name: Laburnum
description: Stunning cascading yellow blooms — but every part of the plant is highly toxic.

name: Camellia
description: The leaves of one species — Camellia sinensis — give us tea.

name: Crape Myrtle
description: Bark peels away in patches to reveal smooth multi-colored trunks underneath.

name: Bottlebrush
description: Australian native whose flowers really do look like the brushes used to clean bottles.

name: Pomegranate
description: Symbol of fertility in nearly every ancient culture from the Mediterranean to the Middle East.

name: Camellia
description: The leaves of one species — Camellia sinensis — give us tea.

name: Rhododendron
description: Some Himalayan species grow into trees over 100 feet tall.

name: Magnolia
description: Older than bees — magnolias evolved earlier and are pollinated by beetles.

name: Cherry Blossom
description: Japan officially tracks the 'sakura front' as blooms sweep north across the country each spring.

name: Rose of Sharon
description: Not a rose at all — it's a hibiscus and the national flower of South Korea.

name: Butterfly Bush
description: A single bush can attract dozens of butterfly species in a single afternoon.

name: Lilac
description: Old shrubs can live and bloom for over a century.

name: Wisteria
description: Can live for centuries; some Japanese specimens have trunks over six feet thick.
